name: Data Lake & Analytics Pipeline
description: Analytics pipeline that ingests data from multiple operational systems, transforms and enriches it using AI/ML models, stores it in a data lake/warehouse, and serves insights through dashboards, reports, and APIs. AI enhances traditional BI with anomaly detection, natural language querying, and automated insight generation.
logisticsContext: Analytics pipelines aggregate shipment data, financial metrics, carrier performance, customer behavior, and market trends. AI-enhanced analytics detect anomalies in billing, identify operational inefficiencies, and generate natural language summaries for executive reporting.

nodes.label: Source Systems
nodes.description: Upstream operational systems that feed raw data into the analytics pipeline. Includes Transportation Management Systems (TMS), Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP), Customer Relationship Management (CRM), carrier API feeds, market data providers, and IoT sensor streams. Data arrives in varied formats (JSON, CSV, EDI, XML) with inconsistent schemas and quality levels.
vulnerabilities: Compromised source system pushing poisoned data that propagates through the entire pipeline undetected
Schema changes in upstream APIs breaking ingestion without alerting downstream consumers
Carrier API feeds injecting manipulated rate data to influence analytics-driven pricing decisions
Sensitive PII or financial data transmitted without encryption from legacy source systems

nodes.label: Ingestion Layer
nodes.description: Batch and streaming ETL connectors that extract data from source systems, perform initial validation, and land raw records into the data lake. Handles Change Data Capture (CDC) for real-time updates, API polling for external feeds, file-based imports for legacy systems, and event stream consumption from message brokers (Kafka, EventBridge).
vulnerabilities: SQL injection or command injection through malformed source data that exploits ingestion parsing logic
Credential exposure if API keys for source system connections are stored in plaintext configuration
Denial of service from sudden volume spikes in streaming sources exhausting ingestion compute capacity
Silent data loss when ingestion jobs fail without proper dead-letter queue handling or alerting

nodes.label: Data Lake (Raw Storage)
nodes.description: Centralized raw data repository storing ingested data in its original format before transformation. Typically implemented on cloud object storage (S3, Azure Data Lake Storage, GCS) with partitioned directory structures organized by source, date, and data domain. Serves as the immutable source-of-truth layer for audit and reprocessing.
vulnerabilities: Overly permissive bucket or container policies exposing sensitive raw data to unauthorized internal users
Lack of encryption at rest for data containing PII, financial records, or contracted carrier rates
Data retention policy violations storing regulated data beyond legally mandated periods
Cross-account access misconfiguration allowing external parties to read or write to raw storage

nodes.label: Transformation Layer
nodes.description: Data processing engine that cleans, normalizes, deduplicates, and enriches raw data into analytics-ready datasets. Implements business logic using dbt models, Apache Spark jobs, or SQL-based transformations. Handles schema evolution, data type casting, currency conversion, timezone normalization, and entity resolution across disparate source systems.
vulnerabilities: Logic errors in transformation code silently producing incorrect aggregations that inform bad business decisions
Unauthorized modification of dbt models or Spark jobs altering how financial or operational metrics are calculated
Resource exhaustion from poorly optimized transformation queries consuming excessive compute and blocking pipelines
Supply chain risk in third-party transformation packages (dbt packages, Spark libraries) introducing vulnerabilities

nodes.label: AI/ML Enrichment
nodes.description: Machine learning models that augment transformed data with AI-generated insights. Runs anomaly detection on financial transactions and operational metrics, classifies shipment delays by root cause, performs NLP-based entity extraction from unstructured carrier communications, and generates predictive features for downstream consumption. Models are trained on historical pipeline data and deployed as batch or near-real-time inference jobs.
vulnerabilities: Training data poisoning if historical pipeline data contains adversarial records that bias model outputs
Model drift causing anomaly detection to miss genuine anomalies or flag excessive false positives over time
Overreliance on AI-generated labels without human review leading to incorrect classifications in reports
Supply chain compromise of pre-trained NLP models downloaded from public model registries
Inference pipeline failures silently falling back to stale enrichment data without notification

nodes.label: Data Warehouse
nodes.description: Structured analytical data store optimized for complex queries and aggregations. Implements star or snowflake schemas with fact and dimension tables for shipment volumes, revenue, carrier performance, customer metrics, and operational KPIs. Typically Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, or Synapse. Serves as the primary query target for BI tools and the NL query interface.
vulnerabilities: Excessive warehouse privileges allowing analysts to query tables containing sensitive financial or PII data
Query injection through dynamically constructed SQL in downstream tools bypassing parameterized query patterns
Cost explosion from uncontrolled ad-hoc queries or poorly written joins consuming massive compute credits
Stale materialized views serving outdated metrics when refresh jobs fail silently

nodes.label: Semantic Layer
nodes.description: Business logic abstraction layer that defines canonical metric definitions, dimension hierarchies, and a governed data catalog. Ensures that 'revenue,' 'on-time delivery rate,' and 'carrier score' mean the same thing regardless of which dashboard, report, or query tool accesses the data. Implemented via tools like dbt Semantic Layer, AtScale, or Cube. Provides the ontology that the NL query interface uses to translate natural language into accurate SQL.
vulnerabilities: Inconsistent metric definitions causing conflicting numbers across dashboards and eroding stakeholder trust
Unauthorized edits to semantic definitions silently changing how KPIs are calculated organization-wide
Incomplete catalog coverage leaving ungoverned tables accessible via direct warehouse queries
Ambiguous dimension hierarchies causing the NL query interface to generate incorrect SQL joins

nodes.label: BI / Dashboard Layer
nodes.description: Visualization and reporting front-end that presents analytics to business users through interactive dashboards and charts. Tools like Tableau, Power BI, Looker, or custom web dashboards. Renders executive summaries, carrier scorecards, financial drill-downs, and operational monitors. Users interact with filters, date ranges, and drill-through paths to explore data.
vulnerabilities: Embedded credentials in published dashboards exposing warehouse connection strings to end users
Dashboard URL manipulation allowing users to access views outside their authorized data scope
Cross-site scripting through unsanitized filter values or custom dashboard parameters
Screenshot or PDF export of dashboards leaking sensitive data outside governed access controls

nodes.label: NL Query Interface
nodes.description: Natural language to SQL translation layer that allows non-technical users to ask business questions in plain English and receive data-driven answers. Uses an LLM to interpret user intent, maps questions to semantic layer definitions, generates SQL queries against the data warehouse, and formats results as tables or charts. Enables questions like 'What was our on-time delivery rate for Q4 by carrier?' without SQL knowledge.
vulnerabilities: Prompt injection through crafted natural language queries that cause the LLM to generate malicious SQL or bypass row-level security
SQL injection via LLM-generated queries that are not properly parameterized before execution against the warehouse
Information disclosure when the LLM reveals table schemas, column names, or data catalog structure in its responses
Excessive resource consumption from LLM-generated queries that produce expensive full-table scans or cross-joins
Hallucinated SQL that appears syntactically valid but semantically joins wrong tables or applies incorrect filters

nodes.label: Automated Report Generator
nodes.description: Scheduled reporting engine that produces and distributes automated business reports â€” weekly carrier scorecards, monthly financial summaries, daily operational snapshots, and ad-hoc executive briefings. Uses LLM-generated natural language narratives to summarize key trends, anomalies, and recommendations alongside charts and tables. Distributes via email, Slack, SharePoint, or embedded portal.
vulnerabilities: LLM-generated narratives hallucinating trends or misrepresenting data that executives act on without verification
Report distribution to incorrect recipients exposing sensitive financial or operational data
Template injection in report generation allowing attackers to embed malicious content in distributed documents
Stale data in scheduled reports when upstream pipeline delays are not reflected in report timestamps

nodes.label: Access Control Layer
nodes.description: Centralized authorization layer enforcing row-level security, column-level masking, and role-based access across all serving components. Controls which users, teams, and service accounts can access which data domains, cost centers, customer segments, and geographic regions. Integrates with corporate SSO/LDAP, implements attribute-based access control (ABAC), and maintains audit logs of all data access events.
vulnerabilities: Role explosion creating overly broad access groups that grant users visibility into data outside their business need
Stale role assignments for departed employees or reorganized teams maintaining access to sensitive pipelines
Bypass through direct warehouse connections that circumvent the access control layer entirely
Insufficient column-level masking exposing PII fields in analytical tables to unauthorized analysts

nodes.label: Data Quality Monitor
nodes.description: Continuous data observability system that monitors pipeline health, data freshness, schema consistency, volume anomalies, and statistical drift across all pipeline stages. Runs automated checks after each transformation step, alerts on SLA breaches, tracks data lineage for impact analysis, and provides a quality scorecard for each data domain. Implements tools like Great Expectations, Monte Carlo, or Soda.
vulnerabilities: Alert fatigue from poorly calibrated thresholds causing teams to ignore genuine data quality failures
Incomplete test coverage leaving critical data domains unmonitored while less important tables are over-tested
Manipulation of quality check definitions to suppress alerts on intentionally corrupted data
Lineage gaps failing to trace data quality issues back to their root cause in source systems

edges.label: Raw data for cleaning and enrichment
Table schemas and metric source data
Query scope restrictions per user role
Source data freshness and volume alerts
Ingestion job metrics and schema snapshots
Row counts, null rates, distribution stats
Table freshness and query performance metrics
